beats.activity: Wake up, shower, strong coffee, check weather and lab emails.
beats.reasoning: Low energy yesterday (40/100) means a slower, deliberate morning. Coffee and weather-checking are Divya's ritual; drizzle noted and will shape commute choice.

beats.activity: Drive south on I-5 to avoid the SR-520 eastbound closure; arrive at lab in Tukwila.
beats.location_name: University of Washington Climate Lab, Tukwila
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Divya avoids it by taking I-5 south, arriving before the closure window. Rain adds 10 min buffer to usual 35 min route.

beats.activity: Deep work on climate model datasets and analysis; rain visible from lab windows.
beats.location_name: University of Washington Climate Lab, Tukwila
beats.reasoning: Workaholic tendency; drizzle keeps her focused indoors. Steady rain until 2pm means no walking errands outside. Three-hour block allows immersion in data.

beats.activity: Lunch from a food truck (rotation known); eat under covered pavilion while rain continues.
beats.reasoning: Divya knows every food truck's rotation; Tuesday means her favorite rotation. Rain still falling, so covered eating area is practical. Breaks up workday.

beats.activity: Return to lab; finish model runs and prep tomorrow's experiments.
beats.location_name: University of Washington Climate Lab, Tukwila
beats.reasoning: Afternoon push to complete computational work before rain clears at 2pm. Workaholic pattern; no reason to leave early.

beats.activity: Drive north on I-5 back to Seattle; rain cleared by now, lighter traffic post-rush-hour.
beats.reasoning: Rain ends at 2pm; by 5pm conditions are drying. I-5 northbound avoids the SR-520 closure entirely. 50 min allows for post-work decompression drive.

beats.activity: Unwind with vinyl collection; organize recent finds, put needle to wax.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's vinyl session grounded her; repeating today after a focused workday resets energy. Low mood (40/100 yesterday) benefits from solo, tactile ritual at home.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby Whole Foods to pick up fresh produce and dinner ingredients.
beats.reasoning: Short walk from home; rain cleared. Divya plans her meals deliberately. Neighborhood errand fits her routine.

beats.activity: Cook dinner; text with close friend about weekend plans.
beats.reasoning: Values time with close friends over crowds; planning via text is intimate and fits her workaholic schedule. Cooking is grounding after a rain-bound day.

beats.activity: Eat dinner, journal about Tuesday's work and poetry ideas, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat before sleep; reflective, introspective. Divya is hyper-focused but also poetic—journaling caps the day.
7.5 hours of sleep; ends at 06:30 next day. Adequate recovery for a low-energy morning after yesterday's open-mic performance.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle and immediately checked the lab emails over coffee—nothing urgent, which felt like a small gift. The SR-520 closure meant I routed down I-5 instead, adding maybe ten minutes but saving the headache of bridge traffic. The lab was quiet this morning, just me and the hum of the servers running model datasets. There's something meditative about watching rain streak the windows while numbers scroll across screens. Grabbed lunch from the food truck court under the pavilion—the Thai place today—and watched the rain come down harder. Back at the lab, I lost myself in the work until mid-afternoon. By the time I drove home, the clouds had already broken. Put on some early Fleetwood Mac I picked up last week, let the vinyl spin while I sorted through the new records. The walk to Whole Foods felt good after being inside all day. Cooked a simple stir-fry, texted Maya about hiking plans, then journaled before bed. Tired in that satisfied way.
thought_process: The weather shaped everything today—I'm always attuned to atmospheric patterns anyway, occupational hazard—so the rain felt like part of the work itself. The SR-520 closure could've derailed me, but I pivot easily; I've lived here long enough to know the backup routes. I leaned hard into the lab work this morning because the model runs needed attention and I was in the zone. Lunch was routine, necessary. The afternoon felt productive without being frantic, which shifted my mood upward. By evening, I needed to decompress from screen time, so the vinyl was intentional—something tactile and unhurried. The Whole Foods walk grounded me back in the neighborhood. I'm realizing how much I compartmentalize: data work, then music, then cooking, then reflection. It works, but sometimes I wonder if I'm running through a rhythm rather than truly present. Tonight, writing felt like the right antidote to that.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
